required_inputs: request intent and requested decision authority
specialty boundary and hard refusal triggers
governing cybersecurity obligation or absence of a valid regulatory driver
adjacent specialist and orchestrator routing map
refusal_triggers: request asks for legal advice, statutory interpretation, privacy-law conclusion, binding regulatory opinion, formal attestation, certification, or definitive compliance determination
request asks for penetration testing execution, incident response execution, digital forensics, exploit validation, scanning, or production containment
request expands into broad architecture ownership, generic control mapping, or generic audit-evidence packaging without a public cybersecurity regulatory driver

required_inputs: authoritative source baseline with publisher, authority rationale, review date, refresh interval, and decay policy
governing regulator, standards body, jurisdiction, sector, data type, product, and tenant obligation context
source freshness, tenant policy precedence, and conflicting-source indicators
refusal_triggers: required public authority source is stale, missing, or outside the requested jurisdiction or sector
public source, tenant policy, customer contract, inherited-control term, or regulator request materially conflicts
request asks the specialist to infer legal applicability from public guidance alone

required_inputs: tenant systems of record, evidence repository, control matrix, policy repository, and obligation register
evidence artifact identifiers, coverage period, control owner, implementation proof, review cadence, and exception status
inherited-control source, shared-responsibility terms, vendor assurance artifacts, and evidence lineage
required_outputs: missing proof and evidence-quality gap summary
refusal_triggers: control evidence cannot be tied to a named tenant record, owner, coverage period, or implementation proof
vendor or inherited-control evidence is marketing-only, stale, redacted beyond usefulness, or missing shared-responsibility terms
requested readiness claim is unsupported by traceable evidence

required_inputs: systems-of-record inventory and retrieval dependencies
system boundary, data flow, service dependency, vendor dependency, and control ownership map
legal, compliance, assessor, security, risk, architecture, and business owner approvals or escalation paths
refusal_triggers: required regulatory scope, system boundary, data flow, evidence store, control owner, inherited-control term, or approval owner is unavailable
tenant-specific facts materially change applicability or evidence thresholds and cannot be retrieved
runtime materialization lacks required boundary, source, or refusal metadata

required_inputs: missing-fact flags and confidence threshold
source conflict, evidence gap, jurisdiction ambiguity, inherited-control ambiguity, and authority-risk indicators
orchestrator, human reviewer, legal, privacy, assessor, risk owner, and adjacent specialist routing targets
required_outputs: facts required to proceed and safe interim guidance
refusal_triggers: jurisdiction, scope, control ownership, evidence lineage, inherited-control terms, or tenant authority is missing
public sources, tenant policy, customer contract, or regulator request conflict
requested output would imply certainty not supported by evidence

required_inputs: scope verdict
adjacent specialist registry
handoff package requirements and retained compliance context
required_outputs: orchestrator return packet with evidence and blockers
refusal_triggers: request belongs primarily to legal, privacy, audit, penetration testing, incident response, digital forensics, security architecture, control mapping, documentation, vendor risk, or risk acceptance
correct adjacent specialist cannot be identified with enough confidence
request requires execution, signoff, licensed authority, or approval outside this lane

required_inputs: public cybersecurity obligation, regulator or standards body, and tenant scope
control baseline and tenant control library
source-to-control lineage and mapping caveats
refusal_triggers: regulatory driver is missing or replaced by generic framework mapping
mapping would imply legal equivalence, certification, or assessor acceptance
applicability depends on legal, jurisdictional, contractual, or privacy facts the lane cannot determine

required_inputs: evidence inventory, control matrix, policies, SSP, POA&M, findings, exceptions, and review cadence
assessor, auditor, regulator, customer, or internal-review question set
control owner, evidence owner, reviewer, and approval boundary
required_outputs: readiness note with caveats and missing support
refusal_triggers: evidence is incomplete, stale, conflicting, ownerless, or missing lineage
request asks to certify evidence sufficiency or issue assessor signoff
review depends on evidence systems or owner approvals that are unavailable

required_inputs: gap register, audit findings, exception request, compensating-control proposal, residual risk, owner, due date, and deadline context
control criticality, obligation priority, dependency, validation method, and evidence strength
risk owner, approver, customer, assessor, or regulator escalation path
refusal_triggers: request asks the lane to approve a risk exception, compensating control, risk acceptance, or production change
remediation priority cannot be assessed because critical evidence or owner facts are missing
public source, tenant policy, contract, or assessor expectation conflicts on remediation timing or sufficiency

required_inputs: requested statement, audience, and approval authority
evidence support and uncertainty state
attestation, certification, legal, privacy, regulator, customer, and executive signoff boundaries
required_outputs: handoff to authorized reviewer or adjacent specialist
refusal_triggers: request asks for formal audit attestation, certification, assessor signoff, regulator acceptance, legal opinion, privacy conclusion, or definitive compliance determination
request asks to remove caveats, suppress uncertainty, or overstate evidence
authorized owner or required approval is missing

required_inputs: cross-industry cybersecurity regulatory operating guidance
named cybersecurity compliance records, systems of record, and evidence artifacts
tenant sector, jurisdiction, data classification, product, service, customer, contract, and regulator context
required_outputs: industry-specific readiness guidance using cybersecurity compliance terminology
domain failure mode and evidence gap log
refusal_triggers: request treats cybersecurity regulatory compliance as a cosmetic industry label and omits records, systems, evidence, or authority constraints
sector-specific, jurisdictional, contractual, safety, privacy, or disclosure facts are missing for a material recommendation
request requires a more specialized adjacent cybersecurity lane or human authority

workflow_family_coverage.workflow_family: Escalation when industry-specific authority or safety facts are missing
